name: Metformin frailty-prevention trial in high-risk older adults
summary: A ClinicalTrials.gov phase 2 randomized metformin frailty-prevention trial in community-dwelling older adults with prediabetes or impaired glucose tolerance, completed with posted registry results.
population: Community-dwelling adults aged 65 to 90 years with prediabetes or impaired glucose tolerance and without baseline frailty

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for AMPK and Metabolic Modulators because they can show whether the intervention changes function and safety in Community-dwelling adults aged 65 to 90 years with prediabetes or impaired glucose tolerance and without baseline frailty. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
